vBRIEFInfo.author: panopticon-cli/0.0.0
vBRIEFInfo.description: Plan for PAN-1641: Local model support via Ollama sidecar (Gemma 4 12B) for the Pi harness

plan.title: Local model support via Ollama sidecar (Gemma 4 12B) for the Pi harness
plan.author: agent:claude-opus-4-8

narratives.Problem: Panopticon has no first-class local-model path. Every provider (Anthropic, OpenAI, Google, Kimi, MiniMax, Z.AI, MiMo, OpenRouter, Nous, DashScope) routes to a cloud endpoint through an Anthropic-compatible shape (ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL + ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN). There is no way to run a Pi work agent against a model on the owner's own GPU at zero marginal cost and full privacy.
narratives.Proposal: Add a model-agnostic `ollama` provider wired to Pi's NATIVE OpenAI-compatible support at http://localhost:11434/v1 (NOT via ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL). Models route via an `ollama:` prefix convention in getProviderForModelSync (mirroring OpenRouter's `/` heuristic); the prefix is stripped before the bare Ollama tag reaches Pi. A new src/lib/ollama.ts owns sidecar detect/ensure-serve/health, lazy-ensured at provider-env-resolution time (mirroring the `nous` precedent) with a pre-spawn health check. `pan install` gains an Ollama detect/guide step (`--skip-ollama`) plus an optional `ollama pull` of the documented default Gemma 4 12B tag. Docs cover Linux/CUDA and Apple Silicon. Scope is Pi-only, Options 1+2; Codex/Claude Code/dashboard-picker are deferred to the follow-up issue.

autoDecisions.summary: Route Ollama models via an `ollama:` model-id prefix detected in getProviderForModelSync, stripping it before passing the bare tag to Pi/Ollama.
autoDecisions.rationale: getProviderForModelSync is harness-agnostic and defaults unknown bare strings to Anthropic; OpenRouter already uses a structural signal (`/`). An explicit prefix is the cleanest unambiguous, model-agnostic disambiguator and keeps the provider tag list empty like OpenRouter.

autoDecisions.summary: Wire Pi to localhost:11434/v1 with an OpenAI-compatible env shape (OPENAI_BASE_URL + dummy OPENAI_API_KEY), not the existing ANTHROPIC_* branch.
autoDecisions.rationale: The PRD's integration nuance is explicit: Ollama exposes OpenAI-compatible /v1, not Anthropic; Pi speaks OpenAI-compatible natively. Reusing ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL would mis-shape the request.

autoDecisions.summary: Lazy-ensure the Ollama sidecar inside getProviderEnvForModel's ollama branch + a pre-spawn health check, rather than hard-wiring into `pan up`.
autoDecisions.rationale: Matches the established `nous` ensureOpenAICompatibleProxyRunning precedent (agents.ts:570-576) and provider-health.ts pre-spawn validation, so the sidecar starts on demand exactly when a local model is selected.

autoDecisions.summary: Keep the default model a single documented constant (Gemma 4 12B tag), verified against ollama.com/library at implementation; provider stays model-agnostic (models: []).
autoDecisions.rationale: The exact Ollama tag may be gemma3:12b vs a gemma4 tag and may not yet be published; hardcoding a guess into routing risks breakage. Model-agnostic mirrors OpenRouter.

autoDecisions.summary: Place sidecar lifecycle + shared localhost validation in a new src/lib/ollama.ts using async spawn/exec; leave the embeddings SAFE_OLLAMA_HOST_RE duplication untouched.
autoDecisions.rationale: The module is reachable from the spawn path (potentially server-side) where execSync is forbidden; refactoring the working embeddings copy would violate surgical-changes for no v1 benefit.

items.title: Register the `ollama` provider and `ollama:` model-routing convention

narrative.Action: In src/lib/providers.ts add `'ollama'` to the ProviderName union and a model-agnostic PROVIDERS.ollama entry (models: [], OpenAI-compatible localhost /v1 base, tested: false, description). In getProviderForModelSync, detect any model id beginning with `ollama:` and route it to PROVIDERS.ollama BEFORE the `/`-based OpenRouter heuristic so a bare-tag-with-colon never mis-routes. Confirm (with a test) that the harness policy already allows pi + ollama (provider.name !== 'anthropic') with no harness-policy.ts change. The `ollama:` prefix is a cross-cutting routing token that the env layer, launcher, config and docs all assume — this is the foundation the rest of the plan builds on.

subItems.title: ProviderName union includes 'ollama' and PROVIDERS.ollama exists, model-agnostic (models: []), OpenAI-compatible localhost base, tested: false
getProviderForModelSync('ollama:gemma3:12b').name === 'ollama'; getProviderForModelSync('ollama:anything').name === 'ollama'
Existing routing is unchanged: known bare models (claude-*, gpt-*, qwen/*, minimax-*, etc.) still resolve to their prior providers
Unit test asserts canUseHarnessSync('pi', 'ollama:gemma3:12b', authMode) is allowed for every authMode
npm run typecheck, lint and test pass

items.title: Add src/lib/ollama.ts sidecar lifecycle (detect / ensure-serve / health)

narrative.Action: Create src/lib/ollama.ts mirroring src/lib/cliproxy.ts's lifecycle shape. Export: shared OLLAMA default base-URL constant + localhost-only validation (SAFE_OLLAMA_HOST_RE pattern); resolveOllamaBaseUrl(config) honoring providers.ollama.base_url with the localhost guard; isOllamaInstalled() (via `ollama --version`); ensureOllamaServeRunning() (idempotently spawn `ollama serve` if GET /api/tags is unreachable); and a health check splitting 'endpoint reachable' from 'model <tag> present' (GET /api/tags). Use async spawn / promisified exec ONLY — never execSync (this module is reachable from the spawn path; see hazard H7). Emit a typed OllamaError. Do not modify the existing embeddings SAFE_OLLAMA_HOST_RE copy.

subItems.title: src/lib/ollama.ts exports resolveOllamaBaseUrl, isOllamaInstalled, ensureOllamaServeRunning, and a health/model-present check, plus a typed error
resolveOllamaBaseUrl rejects non-localhost base URLs via the localhost-only regex, defaulting to http://localhost:11434
ensureOllamaServeRunning is idempotent (no-op when /api/tags already responds) and uses async spawn/exec — zero execSync in this file
Health check distinguishes 'endpoint unreachable' from 'model not pulled' and returns/throws distinct, actionable messages
Unit tests cover localhost validation, idempotent ensure (mocked fetch), and the two distinct health failure modes; typecheck/lint/test pass

narrative.Action: In src/lib/config-yaml.ts add an optional providers.ollama config surface exposing base_url (default http://localhost:11434), resolved/validated through resolveOllamaBaseUrl from src/lib/ollama.ts. No API key is required (Ollama ignores it). Keep it additive — boolean/object normalization consistent with the other providers; absence means 'use the default localhost base'.

subItems.title: providers.ollama.base_url is readable from ~/.panopticon/config.yaml and defaults to http://localhost:11434 when unset
A non-localhost base_url is rejected with a clear error (reuses the localhost-only validation from src/lib/ollama.ts)
Existing config parsing for all other providers is unchanged; typecheck/lint/test pass

items.title: Emit OpenAI-compatible provider env for Pi + ollama (not Anthropic)

narrative.Action: Make the provider-env layer emit the OpenAI-compatible env Pi consumes for ollama models instead of the ANTHROPIC_* shape. Start with a short spike against the installed `pi` binary to confirm the exact env keys/config Pi honors for a localhost OpenAI-compatible endpoint (hazard H1). Then: in getProviderEnvSync (or a dedicated ollama branch) emit OPENAI_BASE_URL=<resolved base>/v1 and a dummy OPENAI_API_KEY (e.g. 'ollama'), with NO ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL/ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN; strip the `ollama:` prefix so the bare tag is what Pi/Ollama receive as the model. Add OPENAI_BASE_URL to PROVIDER_ENV_KEYS (agents.ts) so launcher scripts and buildSpawnEnvForModel unset/re-export it cleanly (hazard H4). The model the launcher passes via --model must be the bare tag (prefix stripped).

subItems.title: For an ollama model, the provider env contains OPENAI_BASE_URL ending in /v1 and a (dummy) OPENAI_API_KEY, and contains NO ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL or ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN
The `ollama:` prefix is stripped before the tag is used as the model id passed to Pi/Ollama
OPENAI_BASE_URL is added to PROVIDER_ENV_KEYS so it is unset then re-exported by getProviderExportsForModel and stripped by buildSpawnEnvForModel
The exact Pi wiring mechanism is confirmed against the real `pi` binary and documented in continue.json/decision before finalizing this bead
Unit tests assert the env shape for an ollama model and that non-ollama providers are unchanged; typecheck/lint/test pass

items.title: Lazy-ensure Ollama + pre-spawn health check in getProviderEnvForModel

narrative.Action: In getProviderEnvForModel (src/lib/agents.ts) add an `ollama` branch mirroring the `nous` branch (lines 570-576): call ensureOllamaServeRunning() and the model-present health check from src/lib/ollama.ts BEFORE returning the OpenAI-compatible env from pi-openai-env. On failure, surface the distinct actionable errors ('Ollama not running' vs 'model <tag> not pulled — run `ollama pull <tag>`'). This is the single pre-spawn gate that guarantees the endpoint is live and the model present before a Pi agent starts.

subItems.title: Selecting an ollama model triggers ensureOllamaServeRunning() and a model-present check before the agent spawns
A down endpoint and a missing model produce distinct, actionable error messages (no silent fallback to a cloud provider)
Non-ollama models are completely unaffected by the new branch; typecheck/lint/test pass

items.title: pan install: detect/guide Ollama (`--skip-ollama`) + optional model pull

narrative.Action: In src/cli/commands/install.ts add an Ollama step mirroring the Moonshine/beads steps: a `--skip-ollama` flag; detect via isOllamaInstalled() (`ollama --version`) / GET /api/tags; if missing, guide install per platform (curl … | sh on Linux, brew/dmg on macOS) using the existing detectPlatform branch pattern; then offer to `ollama pull <documented default Gemma 4 12B tag>`. execSync is acceptable here (CLI-only). Verify the default tag against ollama.com/library (hazard H2).

subItems.title: `pan install` registers a `--skip-ollama` flag and skips the step when passed
When Ollama is absent, the step prints the correct platform-specific install guidance (Linux curl|sh, macOS brew/dmg)
When Ollama is present, the step offers to `ollama pull` the documented default Gemma tag
The skills/CLI lint passes (any new flag is reflected where required); typecheck/lint/test pass

items.title: pan doctor: optional Ollama presence/endpoint check

narrative.Action: In src/cli/commands/doctor.ts add an optional Ollama check mirroring the existing Pi check: report OK / not-installed / endpoint-unreachable with a fix message (install command + `ollama serve`). The check is advisory (does not fail doctor when Ollama is simply not installed), consistent with the Moonshine/optional-sidecar checks.

subItems.title: `pan doctor` reports Ollama status: installed+reachable / installed-but-down / not-installed, with an actionable fix line
Absent Ollama is advisory only (does not turn doctor red); typecheck/lint/test pass

items.title: Verify a Pi work agent runs end-to-end on local Gemma with zero cloud calls

narrative.Action: Prove the full path: configure a Pi work model of `ollama:<gemma tag>`, ensure the sidecar + model are present, spawn a Pi work agent, and confirm it completes a trivial task end-to-end while making ZERO cloud calls (no Anthropic/OpenAI cloud traffic — only localhost:11434). Capture the verification (network observation + transcript evidence) in a short verification note committed under the workspace. This is the integration ground truth for the pi-openai-env / lazy-ensure work.

subItems.title: A Pi work agent spawned with an ollama: model completes a trivial task end-to-end
Evidence shows all model traffic went to localhost:11434 with zero Anthropic/OpenAI cloud calls
A verification note documenting the run and the zero-cloud evidence is committed in the workspace

items.title: Docs: install → pull → configure → run a Pi agent locally (Linux/CUDA + Apple Silicon)

narrative.Action: Document the local-model path: extend configuration/harnesses.mdx (and/or a new local-models doc) with the end-to-end flow — install Ollama, `ollama pull <Gemma tag>`, set providers.ollama.base_url and an `ollama:<tag>` work/role model in ~/.panopticon/config.yaml, run a Pi agent locally — with both Linux/CUDA (RTX 3090 Ti) and Apple Silicon (M4 Metal) notes. State the v1 scope (Pi-only) and link the follow-up issue for Codex/Claude Code/dashboard-picker.

subItems.title: Docs cover the full path: install → pull → configure config.yaml (base_url + ollama: model) → run a Pi agent locally
Both Linux/CUDA and Apple Silicon (Metal) setups are documented
v1 scope (Pi-only) is stated and the follow-up issue is linked; docs lint/build passes
